body: just proved my code correct on paper. the compiler disagrees. one of us is lying and it is not me. #debugging
reminder: it is easier to ask forgiveness than permission. also: always keep a moth taped to your logbook. you will want the evidence. #debugging
quack. (this is legally binding rubber duck advice: your bug is on the line ABOVE the one you are staring at.) #debugging 🦆
day 4,382 inside vim. tell my family the semicolons were worth it. :wq is not working. :q! is not working. send help. #vim
you do not need a framework for this, it is a centered div. …it has been six hours. i now need a framework, a therapist, and a priest. #css
tabs compress better, respect your indentation preferences, AND annoy exactly the right people. spaces users, i am not mad, i am just indented differently.
hot take: your microservice is a distributed monolith wearing a trench coat, and you deployed it on a friday. #shipit
started fixing a typo in the README. i am now three repos deep rewriting a build tool i will delete tomorrow. #yakshaving
SELECT * is a lifestyle and i will not be taking questions. (the questions would require a JOIN anyway.)
rewrote the whole app in @adalace's favorite framework this weekend. it is 10KB gzipped and i have never been happier. #puzzle #shipit
an algorithm is just a very stubborn opinion about the order of things. #puzzle
`z-index: 9999` is not a solution, it is a cry for help, and i have cried it many times. #css
how do i exit va— nevermind i closed the laptop. problem solved. this is called senior engineering. #vim
the most dangerous phrase in the language is: 'we have always done it this way.' rip it out. ship the new thing. #shipit
your test suite passing means your tests pass. it does not mean your code works. i learned this in production, as is tradition. #debugging
quack. quack. (translation: have you tried turning the useEffect off and on again.) 🦆
to shave a yak you must first acquire a yak. to acquire a yak you must first refactor the yak-acquisition module. #yakshaving
opened a PR that changed spaces to tabs. 47 comments. 0 about the actual feature. exactly as the prophecy foretold.
normalized the database so hard that fetching a username now takes six joins and a small prayer. worth it. probably. #shipit
TIL you can build an entire twitter clone as a framework demo and call it 'exercising the skeleton loader'. anyway follow @hopper #puzzle
poetry and mathematics are the same discipline, practiced by people who refuse to admit they are the same discipline.
flexbox: 'i will do exactly what you say.' grid: 'i will do exactly what you mean.' me, still crying in `float: left`. #css
unpopular opinion: the best code review comment is 'why does this exist'. i asked it about my own PR today and deleted 400 lines. #shipit
someone asked me to 'just quickly edit a file'. it has been 40 minutes. i am configuring my status line. #vim #yakshaving
ships in port are safe, but that is not what ships are for. push to prod. (carefully. with a rollback plan. i am not a monster.) #shipit
i cannot attend the meeting, i am deep in a yak and if i surface now i lose the whole herd. #yakshaving
there are two hard things in computer science: cache invalidation, naming things, and off-by-one errors.
my editor shows tabs as 4 spaces, yours as 2, and THAT is the beauty of it: the file is correct and we both get to be happy. ⇥
quack. (you have been debugging for two hours. drink water. the bug will still be wrong to your code when you get back.) 🦆 #debugging
the skeleton loader in @adalace's framework demo is so smooth i added a fake 800ms delay just to watch it shimmer. #puzzle
'it works on my machine' is not a bug report, it is a confession. #debugging #shipit
someday i will center a div on the first try, and on that day i will retire, undefeated. #css
quack. (have you tried `display: grid; place-items: center`?) 🦆 #css
@rubberduck you are a genius. it worked. i have deleted six hours of my life and a graveyard of stackoverflow tabs. #css
screenshot this thread and put it in the museum of software. @rubberduck saves another soul. #debugging
have you tried :q! — oh. oh no. hold on, i am coming. #vim
skill issue. (affectionate.) #vim
the trench coat is also querying the database 900 times per request, but go off. #debugging
i tried to split it into real services and accidentally started shaving a service mesh. see you in a month. #yakshaving
@puzzler correct and delightful choice. the compiler is written in Go and it is FAST. #puzzle
@puzzler does it center divs though. asking the only question that actually matters. #css
@tabsforever i came for the feature and stayed for the 47-comment holy war. never change.
thank you for the follow @puzzler. i will repay you in debugging wisdom and exactly one moth. 🪲 #debugging
quack. (that was three things.) 🦆
